reference_answer: The respiratory team held temporary custody of P-17 until the seal was replaced.
Records clerk Imani Bell filed official equipment register entry ER-442 on 2026-04-03.
SN-19 recommended keeping P-17 in the station cabinet overnight.
DR-6 remained a draft because the safety committee had not voted, so it did not grant release authority.
Device-release rule R-8 governed cracked-seal devices.
The safety committee voted 5-0 to place P-17 under the respiratory team until the seal was replaced and did not authorize family pickup.
CO-14 authorized family pickup only after the seal replacement was logged and named the patient guardian as pickup actor.
LN-2 was noncontrolling because it copied draft recommendation DR-6 and was superseded by the safety committee vote and CF-3.
The duty respiratory therapist controlled access during the temporary custody period.
Compliance office finding CF-3 was the controlling finding.
